chapter_title.ar: باب أَنَّهُ لَا حَدَّ لِمَنْ لَا حَدَّ عَلَيْهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 56 – There is no Penalty (Hadd) for the one upon whom there is no Penalty (Hadd)

text: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) يَقُولُ لَا حَدَّ لِمَنْ لَا حَدَّ عَلَيْهِ يَعْنِي لَوْ أَنَّ مَجْنُوناً قَذَفَ رَجُلًا لَمْ أَرَ عَلَيْهِ شَيْئاً وَ لَوْ قَذَفَهُ رَجُلٌ فَقَالَ لَهُ يَا زَانِ لَمْ يَكُنْ عَلَيْهِ حَدٌّ .

en.hubeali: Ibn Mahboub, from Abu Ayoub, from Fuzeyl Bin Yasaar who said,

‘I heard Abu Abdullahasws saying: ‘There is no Penalty (Hadd) for the one upon whom there is no Penalty (Hadd), meaning, if an insane person were to slander a man, Iasws do not see anything upon him, and if a man were to slander him, so he says to him, ‘O adulterer’, there would be no Penalty (Hadd) upon him (either)’.
